title: ‘Peanuts’ Music Owner Sues Interior Department and 3 Companies for Copyright Infringement
summary: The owner of the “Peanuts” catalog would really like it if companies and the U.S. government stopped using its music without permission.

title: Meta Settles a School District’s Social Media Addiction Lawsuit
summary: Snap, TikTok and YouTube had already settled with the Kentucky district, allowing the companies to avert the first in a series of federal trials.

title: Samsung Averts a Walkout With Big Bonuses, but Discord Over A.I. Profits Brews
summary: Samsung Electronics, a global memory chip supplier, has been a major beneficiary of the A.I boom. It has also become the center of a debate over how to divide its spoils.

title: Trump Cancels Signing of Executive Order Granting Oversight of A.I. Models
summary: The president said he postponed the executive order, which would give the government power to evaluate A.I. models before their release, over concerns about “aspects of it.”

title: Gov. Gavin Newsom to Sign Executive Order Aimed at A.I. Job Loss
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom issued an executive order to explore an overhaul of labor policies to deal with potential mass job displacement from artificial intelligence.

title: Trump Approved a Nvidia Chip for Sale in China. Beijing Doesn’t Want It.
summary: The U.S. tech giant’s powerful H200 chip seemed poised to boost Beijing’s A.I. ambitions, but not a single one has been purchased in China.

title: Bluesky Says Kremlin Is Hacking Its Platform to Spread Propaganda
summary: The company said it was fighting Russian efforts to hijack real users’ accounts to post fake content, an apparently novel tactic.

title: Musk’s SpaceX Reveals Its Finances for the First Time as It Readies for IPO
summary: Mr. Musk’s rocket and satellite maker disclosed its financial performance as it prepares to go public in what is set to be one of the largest offerings to date.

title: Soundtrack to 8,000 Job Cuts: A Meta Worker’s Layoff-Themed A.I. Songs
summary: On a dark day of layoffs at Meta, one employee responded by creating an internal radio station that plays songs about job cuts — generated by artificial intelligence, of course.

title: Meta Lays Off 8,000 Employees, as A.I. Casualties Mount
summary: Employees have signed petitions against being tracked by A.I. and were trying to figure out who had been let go on Wednesday, as the Silicon Valley giant tries to transform into an A.I.-first company.

title: OpenAI Prepares to File for an I.P.O. in Coming Weeks
summary: OpenAI would be one of the most highly anticipated potential initial public offerings, in what is set to be a major year of I.P.O.s for Silicon Valley.

title: Elon Musk Loses $150 Billion Suit Against OpenAI and Sam Altman
summary: A nine-member jury found that Mr. Musk had waited too long to sue. The setback for the tech mogul frees OpenAI to continue in the artificial intelligence race.

title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
summary: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established manufacturers.

title: How Google Is Starting to Win the A.I. Race
summary: Despite its early stumbles, Google’s Gemini has leapfrogged ChatGPT in relevance and usefulness. Soon, it will be ubiquitous.

title: Bipartisan Bill Would Impose New Annual Fee on Electric Vehicles
summary: A House transportation bill introduced this week would require owners of electric cars to pay $130 to cover the cost of road repairs.

title: Senate Republicans Introduce Funding Measure to Combat Online Child Abuse
summary: As part of the reconciliation bill, the senators have allocated $108 million over three years to add nearly 200 specialists at the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Peter G. Neumann, Who Warned of Computer Security Risks, Dies at 93
summary: For decades, he criticized the industry’s lax attitudes toward computer security and individual digital privacy. He also developed solutions.

title: Rising Energy Costs and Data Centers at Heart of NextEra’s Dominion Bid
summary: NextEra’s proposed acquisition of Dominion Energy comes as Americans are paying a lot more for electricity, and data centers are demanding a lot more power from utilities.

title: As OpenAI Celebrates Court Win Against Musk, Other Challenges Lie Ahead
summary: A jury’s rejection of Elon Musk’s $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI was a major hurdle crossed. But the maker of ChatGPT faces a list of other problems.

title: Meta Reassigns 7,000 Employees to Focus on A.I.
summary: The company announced the changes two days before it plans to lay off 10 percent of its work force, or about 8,000 employees.

title: After Elon Musk’s Court Loss Comes the Long Hot A.I. Summer
summary: Even as protests increase, the collapse of Mr. Musk’s suit against OpenAI and Sam Altman will speed up the artificial intelligence juggernaut.

title: Five Takeaways From the Blockbuster Trial Pitting Elon Musk Against OpenAI
summary: It took a jury less than two hours to decide that Mr. Musk had waited too long to sue. But the testimony over three weeks was still illuminating.
